title: A good tool for building your product roadmap
reviewText:
I like the roadmap view you can share with others - it's nice that you are able to select which product features to include in this view and which to leave out. 

I also like the auto-push feature to JIRA, this comes in handy and engineering can choose to just look at the JIRA for the spec instead of two different places. 
I find it confusing the way it's organized, with different sections and sub-sections. There is a steep learning curve but with frequent use it becomes easier to navigate. 

I also don't like how you cannot link a product feature to multiple initiatives, I believe it currently just allows you to assign it to a single initiative.

I would love if Aha! included an integration with Balsamiq, as this is the tool we are currently using for creating wireframes. 
- Steep learning curve, but great as a visual roadmap tool
- Very easy to move features between different releases (drag and drop)
- JIRA auto-push is a very helpful feature
- Does not have an integration with Balsamiq
- Cannot link features to multiple initiatives
Trying to improve the way we organize and plan our product roadmap. We were previously using Confluence for this, but it was a very manual process and there was no easy clean way to view the entire roadmap in one view. This is a lot easier with Aha! where you can see the roadmap, divided by each product and the features within each product.  A good tool for building your product roadmap
switchedReason:
Using Confluence for roadmap planning was a very labor intensive process - there was no easy way to display all the roadmap information in one go, which is why we switched to Aha! just for the roadmap and spec portion. It's also great how you can easily categorize items within the Parking Lot. 

However, I found building out pages on Confluence was a lot easier than it is on Aha!, in terms of the authoring tools and macros available. I also liked the integration of Balsamiq with Confluence, as I use this tool frequently to create wireframes as a part of my specs.

title: Extremely Powerful, Customizable, and API-Rich
reviewText:
Aha is exteremely powerful and customizable. In case the enterprise license is used, the customization possibilities and advanced features are awesome. It is possible to really tailor the product behaviour to your personal needs. Additionally, the product is rich of APIs which allow to extend the integration possibilities even more.
The integration with DevOps is extremely efficient.
Very user friendly and the different views can be tailored for the different stakeholders.
It is also extremely powerful for the part to communicate with clients: the Ideas portal is awesome.
The planning part is lacking some possibilities to tailor the functionality to the very bit and pieces of a complex planning
With Aha I'm able to have a full overview of the state of all the products and releases.
I can customize the details based on my specific workflows and needs, and I can make a fluent communication with my customers. Extremely Powerful, Customizable, and API-Rich

title: Excellent strategy and roadmap features with multiple customizable reports
reviewText:
The tool is focused on high level strategic planning, as opposed to detailed project and task planning, but also offers a way to link the two. For us, the Initiatives, Goals, Scorecard and Releases features look very promising for us to achieve the strategy tracking improvements we are after.

The first thing that drew our attention to Aha! was the varied selection of reports available out of the box, most of which are customizable. The ability to group products into product lines and create report views by these is a very strong feature. This allows us to create views for specific teams, tribes or entire portfolio.

We have not tried the Jira integration but we think that will allow us to keep our detailed planning in Jira, while adding a more consolidated high level view of things.

As a SaaS solution it is easy to get started without much upfront cost and effort. Some of the alternative tools we considered reusing from our existing portfolio required more config and setup to implement the portfolio we want to manage.

We were able to build out our portfolio on Aha! pretty much on our won without contacting the Aha! support team. We plan to use their help for the more advanced features. The support team has been reaching out to us periodically during the trial period.
Some mid-level user documentation might be useful. If it exists, I did not find it easily, but there are a few good intro videos and you can always ask the support team on specific questions.

More info on the report options and capabilities would be useful. We learned by clicking around to see what happened. But we found some really good reports and some that didn't work so well because we were missing part of the data setup.

I noticed some minor UI slow down after using it for a while.
Have a good idea of what portfolio you want to model. If this is clear, putting into the Aha! tools is fairly quick and easy.
After using it as a trial we believe this tool has a lot of potential for us for managing an IT application portfolio.

We were able to model our PLM IT application portfolio in the way we manage it by using product lines, initiatives and goals. We strongly believe that Aha! will help us manage our PLM strategy at a high level, with idea and initiative tracking, without the project planning details we already have in Jira, but plan to use the integration to link the two levels of planning.

We aim to reduce the time we spend manually maintaining strategic roadmaps in Power Point and Excel, and use instead the time to analyze our portfolio more effectively by using the various ways Aha! reports and pivots. 

We also plan to use the scorecard feature, to track business value on our roadmap items. This will be an improvement to how we do strategic planning in our team.
 Excellent strategy and roadmap features with multiple customizable reports

title: Aha is hands down the best Product Management tool out there
reviewText:
The comprehensiveness of its feature set. You can use Aha to document product strategy and business goals, feature and initiative roadmaps, capture and assess ideas, and produce reports and presentations. The integration with JIRA is superb. You can send your features to JIRA, they go through the development workflow, and when they are shipped they update in Aha. 

Aha is clearly a product designed and built from a complete understanding of what product managers want to do and should be doing, and it helps propel a product manager towards the latter. It actually makes you better at your job.

The ability of the admin (me) to specify the views that reviewers and viewers have access to is good but could be better.
Take the time to set it up properly and understand it. You will be well rewarded. 

The holy grail of any product design is effortless onboarding, almost sleepwalking through core loops of the product, and reaching a useful and active state without any discernible effort. This is hard to achieve, and impossible with something as complex as Aha. So be alert, be mindful, dedicate 4 uninterrupted hours to learning and setting up Aha. It will be worth it, trust me.  
Aha is all of our Product Management processes, tools and documentation in a box. It seems robust and comprehensive enough, without being inflexible, so we can use it to run our desired processes, rather than shoe-horning our processes into the tool. Maybe we are just lucky that our structure maps to Aha's structure, but I doubt it. This seems like a really well thought out piece of software. Aha is hands down the best Product Management tool out there
switchedReason: The solution we switched from had very good roadmap visualisation, but little else. Updating it felt like a chore.

title: Aha! is hands-down the most versatile roadmap and idea management platform I've used
reviewText:
Customization
Aha lets you customize everything in their application, from changing in-app terminology to adding a slew of custom fields. It's versatility has been the best thing about it. I'm literally able to create any view I need to create. 

Views
Aha recognizes that a product manager's primary duty is communication. I love that I'm able to create an unlimited number of views that I can custom tailor to my audience. It has significantly reduced the number of questions I get. I can't understate this enough. Any tool that gives you time is worth your investment. Aha gives me time. It helps me do more with less. 

Integration 
Aha's JIRA integration is elegant and effortless. It syncs both ways, supports custom fields, everything I could want. 

Notebooks
I love that I'm able to embed notebooks. That allows us to iframe our roadmaps and other info into our intranet. This is really huge for us.
Notebooks
Some of the reports you can generate are really cool, but there's this weird title page that appears on every one. You can't disable it, and that can be kind of annoying. The nav (backward/forward) is also really subtle (at the bottom of the screen). It still throws me off, and I'm used to it. The combination of a subtle nav and big white title page that might make people think the report failed to load.

Project Planning
The milestones and dates are great, but they end up being inaccurate because they don't seem to skip weekends. It throws everything off. I'd love to be able to sync this with Smartsheet, or even replace Smartsheet, but I'm not able to at this time.

Adding Columns
As I mentioned earlier, you can create any view you like.  Aha used to have a really simple way of adding columns. They recently enhanced it to support more complex views, but the new column sorter is bulky and tedious. It's not the end of the world, but it has made modifying grids much more difficult.
Go to every page and look around. There's a lot you can do. If you don't use everything, that's okay. We don't use every feature, and we're still really happy with the product.
At first, we purchased Aha as a means of organizing all of our feature requests we were getting. It exceeded our expectations in that category. As we became more and more comfortable with the product, we've used it for other things. We recently began working on a massive project with hundreds of moving parts (literally). I needed a way to track them individually, and generate views that I could easily share across all internal teams. I was so successful in doing this, that Aha has become the go-to tool for all pre-dev planning. We do all of our planning and estimating in Aha, and when work is ready to start we push it to Jira.  Aha! is hands-down the most versatile roadmap and idea management platform I've used
switchedReason: It's not that ProdPad is a bad product, by any means. We switched because Aha enabled us to customize the product to our company. They did this from the start. To be fair, I haven't used ProdPad in a couple of years.

title: Aha! allows us to plan, track and communicate our corporate strategy through our product roadmap.
reviewText:
Aha! allows us to easily track features and releases against our top-level corporate objectives. Additionally, we can easily communicate our plans for both internal and external audiences with both objective-based and timeline-based perspectives. The integration with Jira means there's less manual work for me to do to keep things updated and communicate the plan.
The per-seat cost is pretty high, so we don't have as many people using Aha! as I would like.  The read-only role is nice, but most people end up just going to Jira instead.
If you manage multiple product or product lines that are technical in nature (hosted software, etc) and you follow agile development and planning principles you should definitely consider Aha!. 
Internal and external communication of our roadmap is very flexible. Many times I've been asked "We need to present our X month product roadmap for ABC to customer XYZ with details on features 1, 2 and 3."   This could have taken days previously, but with the content already in Aha! and the flexible reporting options I can now do it in less than an hour.

I get to focus in Aha! planning releases, writing user stories and expanding on requirements without having to get into the weeds of scheduling and assignment.  All of that stuff flows seamlessly to Jira for our agile development planning and then updates come right back to Aha!. Aha! allows us to plan, track and communicate our corporate strategy through our product roadmap.
switchedReason: Aha!'s features and design mapped almost exactly to how we run our business. It had all of the basic features we needed, is very easy and intuitive to use and integrates with our existing systems and workflows.

title: Powerful Collaboration Platform with AI, Custom Workflows, and Easy Roadmapping
reviewText:
I love this platform  for its capability to collaborate and build strategy for developmental goals of the organization. This almost have all needed features building plans and making road maps for reduction of risk and improvement of productivity of the organization. I love the AI feature of this platform with capability to assist, search, filter and automate. The security of this platform is also reliable. I also love this platform for its capability to  customize workflow and workspaces. Other than this I also love this platform for its whiteboarding and brainstorming capabilities which improves co-ordination and collaboration among teams. The transition from ideas to road map is made easy with this platform. The notifications and meeting feature of this platform is also worth mentioning. I felt that that the onboarding was really easy as it could be done with a google account. The pricing in comparison with the feature is moderate.
I love most part of this platform but I wish that more improvement is made to make the platform feel less heavy. Other than this for the user may need some training for improve efficiency in using this platform. Other than this I also wish that there was more seamless integration capability of this platform.
This platform actively assist in building strategy and preparation of roadmap. It improves collaboration among teams and improve productivity. Powerful Collaboration Platform with AI, Custom Workflows, and Easy Roadmapping

title: Great for Sharing Feature Status and Comments, but Hard to Manage at Scale
reviewText:
We can create features, share the numbers with clients, and post comments for others. The product also lets us mark the status.
It’s easy to lose the overview when there are more than 2k features, and it’s hard not to create duplicates.
Overview of all feature requests from Sales, CS, Support, and Product. Great for Sharing Feature Status and Comments, but Hard to Manage at Scale

title: Intuitive and Integrated Tool with Stellar Support
reviewText:
I use Aha! Enterprise + Ideas as a product manager, and I love that it allows me to have clarity on the product roadmap and easily communicate it to stakeholders. It's easy and intuitive to use, which is a huge plus for me. The support I received during onboarding was really helpful, and I value the idea tool for its ease in collecting and working on new inputs. The connection with Jira is particularly beneficial, as it makes the product roadmap and development process feel integrated.
The seat pricing, would be good to have different options to allow some profiles to have access to it.
Aha! provides clarity on our product roadmap and helps me easily communicate it to stakeholders. Intuitive and Integrated Tool with Stellar Support

title: Highly Customizable and Efficient with Some Automation Drawbacks
reviewText:
I like that Aha! is highly customizable and allows me to enter the data I need to keep track of my projects. The automation engine in Aha! is really helpful for keeping everybody informed, especially our stakeholders. Additionally, I found the initial setup to be not complicated at all. Overall, it's a very good product for project management and product management, as it helps in publishing roadmaps, sharing ideas, receiving them from our clients, and organizing the work to be done in a support company.
Well, the fact that some custom field types, like notes, cannot be used in automation is a drawback. Basically, what I mentioned about the notes is the only field I have that is multiline, and I can enter formatted text. But I cannot use it in automation, which is an issue for me.
I use Aha! to customize forms and track project data, keeping stakeholders informed with its automation engine. Highly Customizable and Efficient with Some Automation Drawbacks

title: Exceptionally Clear and Intuitive Product Management Tool for entire SDLC
reviewText:
The concepts are presented very clearly, with no ambiguity about what each element represents—a feature is distinctly a feature, a requirement is unmistakably a requirement, and so on. Product management within the tool is straightforward and intuitive right from the start, yet it also offers the flexibility to explore more advanced features as needed. This tool was obviously designed with the entire software development lifecycle in mind, rather than simply being an overblown bug tracking system like some other well-known options.

On the few occasions I have need help with the tool the Customer Support has been stellar in their knowledge/understanding and response times.
you should be able to track a bug as a bug, tied to a specific feature.  Creating a bug as a new feature and linking it back to the original feature loses something in the process and makes it difficult to see how teams/users are doing.
We rely on Aha throughout our entire software development lifecycle, starting from the initial product concept and business case, through defining features, and all the way to assigning and implementing developer tasks.  We replaced Jira with it and have very happily never looked back Exceptionally Clear and Intuitive Product Management Tool for entire SDLC
switchedReason: Jira is overly complex to set up, costs more than the value it delivers, and has lost its innovative edge. You basically need a full time person to manage configuration and setup.   In contrast, Aha has all of the core "bits" that are needed to manage complex products, projects and development cycles out of the box.

title: Visual Roadmap Enhances Team Coordination
reviewText:
I appreciate Aha! for providing a visual roadmap that helps everyone understand the priorities easily. I like that all requirements, dependencies, and goals are in one place. It is beneficial that our stakeholders can enter and manage their ideas, turning them into actionable items. Aha! gives me a high-level view of what's coming in each release, allowing me to quickly see timelines, priorities, and dependencies all in one spot, which helps me plan to complete my documentation work early. It saves me from having to chase PMs or engineers for clarification. The initial setup of Aha! was easy as well.
Custom dashboard and report take more time. More different type templates would save time. When handling a big portfolio it takes time for loading. Speed up loading for the dashboard with large datasets.
Aha! helps us track roadmaps and release plans, link feature dependencies, and keep everything on the same platform for easy access. It also provides a visual roadmap to understand priorities, which helps in planning and completing documentation early without chasing team members. Visual Roadmap Enhances Team Coordination

title: Flexible and Feature-Rich Roadmapping
reviewText:
I find Aha! to be a great tool that fits our enterprise roadmap needs well with strong features and customization. I like how flexible it is, letting us tailor information for different interest holders. The data model is something I do like because it allows me to group work effectively and link it to goals and initiatives. The integration option is a big plus because it provides a real view of work status. As a new user, it seems user-friendly, making it easier to adopt and the initial setup was easy. I appreciate the roadmap options and flexibility, and I value the ability to connect work with goals, initiatives, and releases, which helps link work to strategy.
Some additional flexibility in how releases link to initiatives - though this might be possible, I'm a new user and still learning. The workspace setup and how it rolls up was a little difficult to follow but otherwise good.
Aha! fits our enterprise roadmap needs with strong customization and flexibility. It helps link work to strategic goals and integrates well for a real view of work status. The user-friendly data model enhances our ability to tailor information for different interest holders. Flexible and Feature-Rich Roadmapping

title: Outstanding Features and Exceptional Customer Service
reviewText:
Combination of the features and incredible customer service.   The features also provide a high level of customization to allow the products to conform to your workflows.
There is a bit of a learning curve but the documentation and customer services helps overcome those challenges
We previously were using several applications for product management and documentation.  Working in multiple products that lack integrations creates a challenging environment for effective development.  We also often found ourselves not documenting features and updates as much as we should, and this led to challenging support and client issues.  With Aha, everything is in one place, with data shared across modules.  This makes it so easy to follow a feature across the lifecycle, from idea all the way through release shipment and documentation.  Aha also provides a great "best practice" framework in their design that we are operating as a better product team just by following their steps.  We've seen productivity and product knowledge increase across our teams as a result. Outstanding Features and Exceptional Customer Service

title: Aha! Unifies Product Management with Roadmaps and Idea Tools
reviewText:
Aha!'s Roadmaps, Ideas, and Discover portals really help to tie together the whole product cycle from ideation through deployment. It's convenient to have everything in one place with the ability to drill down from something on the roadmap to trace back what factors went into prioritizing that item over others. "Ideas" has good brainstorming tools like Whiteboards and Prioritization. It keeps track of history on the Idea record, so if that Idea ends up being chosen and scheduled into the Roadmap, you can always go back in and see if people voted for it, commented on it, and what other items or artifacts within Aha! it relates to. There are several different view options, so the Roadmap can be presented in the fashion that best fits your audience. The web link sharing option is helpful so that people without Aha! access can have an up-to-date view of the Roadmap accessible at any time.
There are many configurable templates and settings, which allows a high degree of customizability, but that complexity also leads to much clicking around to find all of the settings and configurations, especially when first learning the tool. Their support is readily available via chat or email or scheduling a call, and they were able to assist quickly and effectively each time we had a question.
We need to keep everything related to each initiative together so that we can easily trace back to the problem it aims to solve, the goals, and supporting data. Aha! Unifies Product Management with Roadmaps and Idea Tools

title: Streamlined Idea Capture and Prioritization with Jira Product Discovery
reviewText:
Jira Product Discovery makes it easy to capture ideas, prioritize them, and collaborate with the team. I like that it connects product planning directly with Jira Software, so moving from an idea to implementation is straightforward. The interface is clean, and the custom views help track priorities without adding unnecessary complexity.
The biggest drawback is the initial learning curve. While it's powerful, it takes some time to understand all the features and configure them effectively. I also wish there were more customization options for views and reporting, and the pricing may be a bit high for smaller teams.
It solves the challenge of managing and prioritizing product ideas from multiple sources. By centralizing feedback and linking priorities to business goals, it helps our team make better product decisions, stay aligned, and move ideas into development more efficiently. Streamlined Idea Capture and Prioritization with Jira Product Discovery

title: Excellent for Product Prioritization and Roadmapping
reviewText:
At LocalConnect, we use Jira Product Discovery to collect customer feedback, create idea records, and prioritize feature requests before each release. I like how custom fields and prioritization make it easy to compare ideas based on customer impact and business value. Once an idea is approved, we link it directly to Jira Software so the development team can start implementation without duplicating work. The integration with Figma also helps us review designs while discussing new features, making product planning much more organized and collaborative.
While Jira Product Discovery is very useful overall, some advanced roadmap views and customization options are only available in higher plans, which can be limiting for smaller teams. When managing a large number of ideas, the workspace can sometimes feel slower, making it take longer to review and prioritize features during planning sessions. I would also like more flexible dashboard customization and reporting so it's easier to share product updates with different stakeholders.
Before using Jira Product Discovery, our product ideas and customer feedback were scattered across different documents and discussions, making prioritization difficult. Now we can collect feedback in one place, score ideas, and align the team before development begins. The integration with Jira Software makes it easy to move approved ideas into development, reducing planning time and helping us make more informed product decisions. Excellent for Product Prioritization and Roadmapping

title: Efficient Idea Management But Needs Better Performance
reviewText:
I use Jira Product Discovery to organize and prioritize product ideas, which helps us prioritize the right features. I like having everything in one place. I value the 'view and filter' features because they help us find information quickly. It was very easy to get started with the setup.
Performance can be slow with large projects.
I use Jira Product Discovery to organize and prioritize product ideas. It helps us prioritize the right feature and having everything in one place. Efficient Idea Management But Needs Better Performance

title: Streamlines User Feedback & Market Research with Effective Idea Scoring
reviewText:
Helps to gather and organise User feedbacks and market research and helps in scoring and comparing ideas
Interface needs to be improve as there is a lag and also integration with Jira needs to be improve
Helps in gathering and managing User feedbacks and market research and helps in scoring and comparing  ideas thus helps in making business decisions Streamlines User Feedback & Market Research with Effective Idea Scoring

title: Fast, Intuitive UI and Clear Visibility — Jira Sync & Customization Fall Short
reviewText:
It offers clear documentation of internal platform improvements, provides everyone with visibility into ongoing progress, and calls out what's at risk. The UI is fast, flawless, and easy to use, so new colleagues begin using it seamlessly from day one of onboarding.
Stakeholders are often confused by having multiple Jira projects as it's difficult to sync ideas between JPD and the Epics in Jira Software. We're using AI to generate all data fields, workflows, and views, but we've found those generated elements to be less customisable than in Jira Software.
We had been using older tools without SSO, whereas JPD is natively integrated with the other Atlassian tools. After adopting it, the whole team agreed that projects seemed to be completed faster. It saves on licensing costs for multiple tools as it has become the new standard. Fast, Intuitive UI and Clear Visibility — Jira Sync & Customization Fall Short
switchedReason: Willingness to try new software and looking forward to get more options on integrations

title: Streamlined Task Management and Team Collaboration
reviewText:
I find Jira Product Discovery incredibly useful for collecting ideas and collaborating with all teams. It enables us to work in parallel, trace tasks efficiently, and prepare detailed product roadmaps. Its connection with Jira makes it very easy to track and complete pending work while seeing the status of ongoing tasks. It's effective, fast, and provides perfect monitoring, making it more traceable. The fast connection with all teams and its simplicity are huge benefits. The setup was very easy and simple, with an easy manual that makes it accessible for anyone to set up.
All is fine just need energetic UI background so it seems very fresh to start working.
I use Jira Product Discovery to trace tickets, collaborate with teams on roadmaps and ideas, work in parallel, and track tasks effectively. Transitioning from Excel, it's fast, traceable, and perfect for monitoring work with seamless team connections. Streamlined Task Management and Team Collaboration
switchedReason: Fast, reliable, and simple. It’s traceable, makes me more productive, and is highly useful.

title: Centralized Ideation, Dynamic Roadmaps
reviewText:
I use Jira Product Discovery for centralizing ideas and insights, which serves as a single, centralized intake funnel for me. I like that it replaces rigid timelines with dynamic, outcome-based roadmaps. Because it is natively built into Jira, these roadmaps are living documents, and when engineering updates a ticket in Jira Software, the status automatically reflects on the Jira Product Discovery strategic roadmap. It's pretty easy to set up.
Jira Ecosystem Lock-in: if your engineering team doesn't use Jira Software for delivery, Jira Product Discovery loses its biggest competitive advantage.
I use Jira Product Discovery for centralizing ideas and insights, making it a single intake funnel. It replaces rigid timelines with dynamic roadmaps. Integrating it with Jira Software keeps these roadmaps updated automatically. Centralized Ideation, Dynamic Roadmaps

title: Streamlined Idea Management, Seamless Engineering Integration
reviewText:
I really appreciate its seamless connection to the engineering pipeline, unlike traditional product tools that require manual translation of ideas into development tickets. The idea prioritization views are the highlight finally have a structured way to rank what actually matters. UI is clean and intuitive, much lighter than regular Jira which is a relief. Integrations with Jira Software are convenient, keeps product and dev teams aligned without extra tools. Performance is smooth, no complaints there. Onboarding was quick, support docs are solid. AI-driven insights for spotting patterns across customer feedback is a nice touch, saves a lot of manual digging. Talking about the pricing it felt pretty reasonable if youre already a preexisting customer of the atlassian
The prioritization views can feel a bit rigid at times, which makes it difficult to customize the scoring so it truly reflects how our team thinks and evaluates priorities.
I use Jira Product Discovery to capture, prioritize, and map ideas before they enter development. It solves messy pre-development issues by seamlessly connecting to the engineering pipeline and bridging the gap between why and how things get built, centralizing insights and feedback. Streamlined Idea Management, Seamless Engineering Integration

title: Transparent, Intuitive Product Discovery with Clear Prioritization
reviewText:
What I like most about Jira Product Discovery is how it helps teams organize ideas, prioritize opportunities based on impact, and make product decisions transparently. It keeps product discovery structured, while still making it easy to collaborate with stakeholders and maintain a clear, shared roadmap.
I don’t have any major complaints. A few of the more advanced customization options could be a bit more flexible, but overall the experience feels intuitive and works well for discovering products.
Jira Product Discovery helps me organize product ideas, prioritize them based on customer value and business impact, and keep stakeholders aligned. It makes decision-making more transparent, cuts down the time I spend managing feedback, and helps the team stay focused on the highest-impact opportunities. Recomend! Transparent, Intuitive Product Discovery with Clear Prioritization

title: Helpful task trackers, but the UI feels lackluster
reviewText:
It’s easy to prioritize tasks using the trackers the software provides. It’s been really helpful for staying organized and keeping everything on track.
The UI is a bit lackluster, and I’d like it to have a little more spice to it.
I’m a very unorganized person, and this helps me a lot by making sure I don’t forget important things. Helpful task trackers, but the UI feels lackluster

title: Streamlined Product Planning with Insightful Roadmaps
reviewText:
I use Jira Product Discovery to organize and prioritize product ideas, features, and improvements across my game dev projects. It helps me manage a large number of ideas, feature requests, and development priorities. I like that it brings product planning and development thinking together in one place, and I find the visibility into roadmaps and priorities especially useful because there are always competing ideas. It creates a shared understanding of what the team is working toward and why, which helps reduce misalignment between design, engineering, and production. The initial setup of Jira Product Discovery was very straightforward.
Maintaining a clean and consistent structure requires ongoing effort, especially when ideas grow. Over time, fields, tags, and prioritization criteria can become unevenly applied unless the team is very disciplined, which adds extra overhead.
I use Jira Product Discovery to organize and prioritize ideas. It helps manage many feature requests and priorities across projects. The visibility into roadmaps reduces misalignment, creating shared understanding among teams. Streamlined Product Planning with Insightful Roadmaps

title: Centralized, Data-Driven Roadmapping That Elevates Product Discovery
reviewText:
I like having a centralized repository, data-driven prioritisation, and clear roadmap visibility. Overall, it’s a platform that genuinely encourages a structured approach to product discovery. It’s the best product management platform I’ve ever used, and it’s packed with advanced functionalities.
As per my job requirements, data is the most crucial aspect for me, and sometimes Jira just doesn’t operate well around data exportation, data aggregation, and advanced analytics.
I would like to mention the main reason we started using Jira, and that is it has resolved the issue of fragmented reporting across product initiatives by providing us with a centralised hub of information, which ultimately improves our data analysis and communication. Centralized, Data-Driven Roadmapping That Elevates Product Discovery

title: A Cohesive Platform for Product Ideas, Discovery, and Delivery
reviewText:
It’s great to have a single platform where we can capture product ideas and opportunities all in one place. I can clearly see the smooth connection between product discovery and delivery, which makes the overall workflow feel more cohesive. The custom views feature is also really helpful, since it lets our teams focus on the information that’s most relevant to them.
Although it’s a great tool and I personally loved using it, some of my stakeholders were hesitant to adopt it and still preferred traditional roadmap tools. It would be helpful if it could better cater to those preferences and make the transition feel easier.
The issue in justifying roadmap decisions have been settled due to the data backed prioritisation that effortlessly increases leadership confidence. The support offered by Jira is also incredible and user-friendly. A Cohesive Platform for Product Ideas, Discovery, and Delivery

title: Everything a Product Manager Needs for Discovery and Roadmaps
reviewText:
It has everything what a product manager needs as a tool for organizing discovery items and roadmaps. You can customize is as you wish.
My team and my stakeholders don’t use it to see updated informations,m.
Collecing ideas to one place, prioritize and adding insights for later reference. Everything a Product Manager Needs for Discovery and Roadmaps

title: Centralized Idea Backlog, but Limited Flexibility and Weak Claude Integration
reviewText:
One place to store the backlog of qualified ideas. It’s in the same ecosystem as the engineering team.
Not very flexible. Claude has taken over a lot of the legwork when it comes to storing and prioritising ideas. The integration with Claude felt weak overall.
Helps us not to lose ideas Centralized Idea Backlog, but Limited Flexibility and Weak Claude Integration

title: Great platform for clear and easy
reviewText:
I really like how easy ProductPlan is to use and how clearly it brings visibility to the roadmap. The platform makes it simple to organize priorities, communicate plans, and keep everyone aligned around the product strategy. It helps turn complex planning into something much more visual, collaborative, and easy to follow.
There isn’t anything specific that I dislike about ProductPlan. Overall, the experience has been very positive, and the platform meets expectations well by being easy to use, clear, and helpful for roadmap visibility and alignment.
ProductPlan helps solve the challenge of keeping product roadmaps organized, clear, and easy to share with different teams and stakeholders. It provides better visibility into priorities, timelines, and strategic initiatives, which helps everyone stay aligned and understand the direction of the product.

The main benefit is that it makes planning and communication much simpler, reducing confusion and making it easier to present the roadmap in a visual and professional way Great platform for clear and easy

title: Great Jira Integration and Visuals, But Roadmap Sync Needs Improvement
reviewText:
Integration with Jira, visuals. We review it once a month, with leadership. For us, it's the visual for leadership, since they won't understand the Jira tickets. It's easy enough to use.
The integration takes time to figure out, what imports work and what doesn't and why. Also, the roadmap is super sensitive, if you just try to move it a bit, everything goes out of whack, and it syncs back to Jira, messing up plans.
It allows us to create several levels of roadmaps for different audiences in the company. Team obviously works on Jira, but middle management need to see the plans for the Quarter and year, and upper management need to understand how all the blocks fit together from different products at different times. Great Jira Integration and Visuals, But Roadmap Sync Needs Improvement

title: Great Roadmaps, Expensive with Some Visual Limitations
reviewText:
I like using ProductPlan for creating project roadmaps to increase clarity and understanding in executing project plans. The roadmap feature is particularly helpful because it enables alignment and clarity in quickly and easily communicating plans and potential milestones. The software can help team members focus on tasks and milestones relevant to their skills, making it easier for them to own their responsibilities and contribute to the project success.  The ease of use is an advantage for me.
I have trouble with the visualization and the size of the text when creating a roadmap mostly for short-duration tasks in a large schedule, and magnification doesn't seem to adequately address this issue which means you really must develop alternate schedule views. ProductPlan lacks critical path determination, which is crucial for my needs. I've had previous issues with invoicing and ensuring the invoice is correct with the necessary information.  That issue was resolved fairly quickly. I find the product feature(s) are expensive compared to other competitive software.
I use ProductPlan to create project roadmaps to increase clarity and alignment by focusing on relevant tasks and milestones, minimizing potential team member overwhelm from extensive details typically provided in a critical path schedule. Great Roadmaps, Expensive with Some Visual Limitations

title: Great for Managing Multiple Roadmaps,  would benefit from bespoke support package to get more
reviewText:
I appreciate that I can combine several roadmaps and dependencies into a single view. This feature makes it much easier to manage and track everything in one place.

It is also easy to move around things when dates and timings change, by drag dropping a bar/container ect.

The colouring system keeps a clean look and allows you to quickly identify task-types.
Over the past three years, we have worked with several different customer success representatives. Each time a new person joins, it becomes challenging to start over, especially since our product is quite complex and large. It often takes considerable time to bring someone up to speed on our operations and requirements.

I believe that Fugro, in particular, would greatly benefit from a more tailored support approach—one that can also partially grasp the technical use cases of our products.
Pulling together many parts of a wider scale product into more granular details. Great for Managing Multiple Roadmaps,  would benefit from bespoke support package to get more

title: Streamlined Strategic Planning Tool powered by a Clean UI, Needs Better Integrations & Reporting/Analytics
reviewText:
I really like the easy-to-use UI of ProductPlan. It has a very clean look and makes navigation easier. It's also quick and easy to find the features that I'm looking for, as it is woven into the experience very thoughtfully. It's not clunky or overly complex. I love the visualization aspect of it and the ability to customize different views. The initial setup was very easy, and I was impressed with the time it took to get it up and running, which was in no time. Onboarding was a great experience as well, and we were all well-versed after just a couple of meetings. The tool is very user-friendly.
The biggest drawback is for its ability to integrate with other systems and databases seamlessly. So a lot of the data entry work seems very manual and time consuming. Some of the advanced features come up with a pretty hefty price tag. It also lacks some pretty basic reporting and analytics functionality.
It aligns every business entity and shared service unit across the same priorities, solving the problem of a unified system of record and source of truth. Streamlined Strategic Planning Tool powered by a Clean UI, Needs Better Integrations & Reporting/Analytics

title: Needs Easier Multi-Project Management
reviewText:
it is good to see all the projects in the department
good for manager with few projects. hard to create multi projects
not much Needs Easier Multi-Project Management

title: Frustrating Integration and Usability Issues
reviewText:
I don't have any particular thing I like. The UI has a modern feel if I have to choose something.
I tried using ProductPlan for maintaining roadmaps, but it's usually horrible to use, so I prefer not to use it. It's virtually impossible to do ad-hoc work because the integration is so poorly done. When I need to update and change the roadmap, if I realize something's missing from Jira, I have to sync it, which takes many minutes instead of seconds. Integrations and usability are not at a level that would satisfy users. Although we have an enterprise license, nobody uses the features because they feel useless. The initial setup was hard.
ProductPlan's integration with Jira is cumbersome and inefficient, making ad-hoc updates slow and impractical. Usability issues and dissatisfying integration levels hinder effective roadmap maintenance. The modern UI stands out positively, but overall features feel useless, and initial setup was challenging. Frustrating Integration and Usability Issues

title: User-Friendly Roadmapping with Strategic Portfolio Insights
reviewText:
The interface is user-friendly, making roadmapping a simple process. I appreciate how features can be connected to company objectives, team goals, and initiatives, which gives leaders a strategic view of their portfolio. This setup enables them to track progress across multiple teams effectively.
The Jira integration has a noticeable gap, as not all mapped fields are updated properly. Additionally, the push and import functions can lead to a significant amount of repetition. It would be simpler if Product Plan leveraged the Jira Epic or Story status (To Do, In Progress, Done/Released).
ProductPlan has been instrumental in helping us establish a standardized roadmap format across our product teams. It has enhanced visibility into our progress toward completing initiatives, improved our ability to track alignment with company-wide objectives, and increased transparency for both our internal and external customers. User-Friendly Roadmapping with Strategic Portfolio Insights

title: Streamlined Roadmapping with Intuitive UI
reviewText:
I use ProductPlan for roadmaps and a little strategy. I like that it provides consistent roadmaps across the organization and lets us focus on the items instead of formatting. I really appreciate the easy-to-use GUI, which includes drag and drop functionality, offers different views, and allows customization and the addition of fields across all roadmaps or on an individual basis.
We are a challenge with pushy containers to ADO. Wish the view would have a setting to automatically roll the view as the date change
ProductPlan provides consistent roadmaps across the organization, allowing me to focus on the items rather than formatting. Streamlined Roadmapping with Intuitive UI

title: Product Plan has been a great experience, easy to use and has made planning organised and efficient
reviewText:
Product plan is very user-friendly and easy to implement as well.
Dependency management feels basic: While you can show dependencies visually, the tool doesn’t offer deeper tracking, alerts, or automated impact analysis
Lack of a structured roadmap: Previously, priorities and timelines were scattered across documents and conversations, making it difficult to get a unified view. ProductPlan centralises everything in one place. Product Plan has been a great experience, easy to use and has made planning organised and efficient

title: Effortless Visibility and Customization, Perfect for Cross-Department Roadmapping
reviewText:
I really like ProductPlan for its ease of use. What stands out to me is that it's user-friendly and easy to understand, so I’ve been able to train myself and other team members effectively, getting them gradually onboard. I appreciate the tutorials that align with what you need to do, allowing you to handle tasks yourself. I love the service provided, as it's helpful whenever we have questions or need guidance. Another thing I appreciate is that there's no clunky configuration and no code is required, which makes everything simple. The interface is straightforward, with everything in one spot, making it user-friendly. I also like the search feature, which makes it easy to navigate. It's very supportive and responsive, especially when we use ProductPlan for presentations; it always presents well and facilitates understanding among team members. The integrations are pretty decent too, particularly the integration with Jira, and we're planning to further use webhooks for more automation. Overall, it makes road mapping straightforward and not overwhelming, providing easy digestibility of information.
I don't like that the search feature doesn't pop results right up in view; you have to scroll up to see it, and it doesn't line up with where you're looking. Also, I'd love to have another date option to compare projected dates to actual, which would benefit leadership when reviewing changes. Being able to copy and paste bars to other roadmaps would also be beneficial. Finally, sometimes integrations with tools like ServiceNow don't always work, which means we can't fully depend on them.
I use ProductPlan to manage projects, provide visibility, and aid in quarterly planning. It simplifies roadmap creation, making them easily digestible and editable, with creative options. It integrates well with Jira, facilitating easier access and less manual work. Effortless Visibility and Customization, Perfect for Cross-Department Roadmapping

title: Effortless Implementation and User-Friendly Experience
reviewText:
It is not complicated! Easy to implement. The ProductPlan team is also always amazing.
I do with there was a bit more customization in certain instances.
We struggle with getting our Product planning into one place. Our ENG team works religiously in Jira, but we lack views and planning tools from a real product standpoint there. Product Plan gives us what we need outside of Jira to be able to visualize and share roadmaps with internal stakeholders like sales and leadership. Effortless Implementation and User-Friendly Experience

title: User-Friendly Tool with Room for PI Planning Enhancements
reviewText:
I love how user-friendly ProductPlan is, as it makes navigating and managing product roadmaps a seamless experience. The tool enhances visibility into features and stories, integrating well with ADO to inform and feed into our roadmaps efficiently. The ease of the initial setup is another aspect I appreciate, as it was very straightforward and hassle-free.
I would like to see more focus around PI planning activities and specifically determining load for future quarters.
I use ProductPlan for product roadmaps, providing visibility into features and stories in ADO. User-Friendly Tool with Room for PI Planning Enhancements

title: Transforms Strategic Planning with Clear, Visual Roadmapping
reviewText:
I really like how ProductPlan gives us a clear, visual way to organize our initiatives. It helps me connect strategy, timelines, and ownership in one place, making it easier to align teams and keep everyone focused on the same goals.
I’d say the main gap is the lack of strong OKR-tracking capabilities, which makes it harder to tie initiatives directly to outcomes. I also see inconsistencies in how teams use tables and timeline views, so standardization across the organization is still a challenge.
ProductPlan brings all our initiatives together in a single location, providing much-needed structure to our planning process and making team priorities clear. By aligning roadmaps, timelines, and ownership, it addresses the issue of scattered information. This alignment not only streamlines coordination but also ensures that our execution remains closely tied to our overall strategy. Transforms Strategic Planning with Clear, Visual Roadmapping

title: Streamlined Roadmap Creation and Communication
reviewText:
I love how ProductPlan enables me to build and communicate roadmaps clearly and visually, turning strategy into an easy-to-understand plan that aligns product, engineering, and leadership teams. It effectively solves the problem of misalignment and lack of visibility around product priorities. I appreciate how the visual roadmap format facilitates clear, quick understanding of priorities, timelines, and direction without the need for lengthy explanations. This makes alignment and decision-making much faster across teams and stakeholders. I particularly value the drag and drop roadmap builder and the ability to create multiple roadmap views for different audiences, which simplifies communication and caters to the diverse needs of stakeholders. Easy sharing features further enhance collaboration and ensure everyone is on the same page. Overall, I find the tool instrumental in providing clarity, organization, and efficiency in managing projects.
I believe ProductPlan could be improved with better dependency management, stronger integrations, and more customizable views for complex roadmaps.
I use ProductPlan to solve misalignment and lack of visibility in product priorities, ensuring clear, visual communication of roadmaps and strategies, aligning product, engineering, and leadership teams. Streamlined Roadmap Creation and Communication

title: Versatile Tool with Seamless Azure DevOps Integration
reviewText:
I like the way we can configure Craft.io to our own company's ways of working. This configuration and the ability to sync across to Azure DevOps are key for us as a business; separating the backlog management in Product Management from the engineering work that takes place is very helpful. The setup was easy.
We would like to utilize AI to help us triage feedback that we receive and help prioritize our backlog more fully. We have recently engaged in their beta trial program for using their MCP and hope that we'll be able to benefit from that and streamline some of our internal processes in the future.
I use Craft.io to gather insights for product innovations with their feedback portal, and for backlog refinement and prioritization, helping to build our roadmap. Versatile Tool with Seamless Azure DevOps Integration

title: Seamless Product Management with Craft.io
reviewText:
I use Craft.io to create and manage features, epics, and user stories for my product management team. It helps us follow product management best practices and collaborate effectively, building products with clear prioritization and planning. Craft.io brings in a lot of discipline that was missing between the product and dev team, and the one-to-one mapping is super helpful. What I like most about Craft.io is its ease of use with out-of-the-box functionalities. It's user-friendly, meaning you don't have to be tech-savvy to learn or use the product. My team adapted to it in just one to two weeks. Another feature I like is the AI built into Craft.io, which helps me understand a feature or user story without getting deeper into the descriptions. And the setup was very easy, with the team being supportive in the onboarding process. It took us just a couple of weeks to fully adapt to its features.
What would make it even better is allowing certain field items to be applicable only to certain types of work items.
I use Craft.io to create and manage features, epics, and user stories, which helps us follow product management best practices, collaborate effectively, and build products with clear prioritization. It brings discipline across teams, offers seamless integration, and is user-friendly and easy to set up. Seamless Product Management with Craft.io

title: Flexible and Streamlined Product Management with Craft.io
reviewText:
I like the flexibility Craft.io offers in defining my own process that best fits the needs of the organisation and being able to create the views needed through each stage of the product lifecycle. The Linear integration also helps reduce friction between product and engineering and minimises the manual updates required to ensure effective customer communication on delivery. The ideas portal is also an important feature, along with the ability to create and publish roadmaps to customers. The initial setup was straightforward, with no real issues.
The customer feedback/ideas portal has great potential though could be more flexible on the reporting side. Would be useful to be able to pull additional data across into the workspace for clear internal communications. Only Linear has an integration. A Slack integration would be useful, and I would love to have a better way of creating and publishing release notes for clients.
Craft.io bridges a gap by introducing a robust product management process, reduces friction between teams, minimises manual updates, and allows flexibility in defining processes. The Linear integration and roadmap publishing features are valuable, enhancing strategic planning and communication. Flexible and Streamlined Product Management with Craft.io

title: Transformative Tool for Product Alignment, with a Gentle Learning Curve
reviewText:
I love how Craft.io has become our single source of truth for product roadmaps and dependency mapping, helping break down silos across teams and making collaboration much clearer. The tool is easy to learn, flexible, and has been especially embraced by our design and UX teams, who now use it daily. The support from Craft.io's team has also been fantastic, always available to answer questions and guide us through the setup.
One challenge we faced was when the dependency lines feature was removed, as we had planned to use it extensively for mapping. Also, our initial setup was overwhelming because we imported all our data at once, which made it harder to iterate and experiment with the tool's capabilities. In hindsight, starting with a small sample would have been better. Some permissions (like needing to be owner to create feedback portals) can also be limiting.
Creating a single aligned Roadmap across our entire Portfolio of Products allows for better planning, easier prioritization and clearer dependency mapping. Transformative Tool for Product Alignment, with a Gentle Learning Curve

title: Connecting strategy to execution in one system
reviewText:
Craft has become our central product system of record. We use it to connect strategy to execution, and it gives visibility across exec, product, engineering, and go-to-market teams without me rebuilding the same story in five different decks.

The roadmap functionality is a big win. I used to spend hours maintaining PowerPoints for different audiences. Now I can tailor views for board, leadership, or customer conversations directly in Craft and know it’s pulling from the same underlying work. That alone has saved a meaningful amount of time.

I also appreciate how flexible it is. We’ve customized fields to align with our intake process, strategic pillars, and value streams, and it supports the way we think about work without forcing a rigid framework. And the Craft team has been strong partners. Our CSM has been responsive and proactive, which matters when you’re scaling product discipline in a growing organization.
The hardest part hasn’t been the tool. It’s the change management. Getting a team to truly treat any system as the single source of truth takes repetition and reinforcement. We saw some usage drop after onboarding and I had to be intentional about tying roadmap reviews and strategic conversations back to Craft.

There’s also still some workflow tuning happening on our side, especially around backlog hygiene and prioritization. Craft gives you flexibility, but that means you need to be clear about your own governance. As our product processes mature, we’re continuing to refine how we use it to support that.
Craft is solving fragmentation for us.

Before Craft, strategy lived in one place, roadmaps in PowerPoint, backlog in Jira, and executive updates somewhere else entirely. It required a lot of manual stitching to tell a coherent story from company priorities down to product work. That made alignment harder than it needed to be.

Craft gives us a single place to connect strategic pillars, product themes, initiatives, and features. It helps me clearly show how our roadmap ties to company objectives and makes it easier to communicate that across executives, engineering, marketing, and customer-facing teams.

The biggest benefit has been clarity and time savings. I’m spending less time recreating slides and more time actually driving prioritization and outcomes. It’s also elevated the quality of product conversations internally because we’re looking at the same source of truth instead of debating which version of a deck is current. Connecting strategy to execution in one system

title: All-in-One Product Management Made Effortless
reviewText:
It brings everything related to product management in one place instead of juggling multiple tools for roadmaps, backlog, strategy, and stakeholder updates. The roadmapping feature is very visual and easy to understand and it helps me work more strategically and make better decisions.
Sometimes it can be a bit slower while handling a large amount of data, and there is also a learning curve in the initial stage, but the customer support was very supportive, which made it easy. Overall, it is an amazing tool
It helps to resolve the problem of having product work scattered across too many tools. Before the implementation of craft.io, things like strategy, roadmaps, prioritization, and execution felt disconnected. Now everything is in one place and which makes it easier for me to see the bigger picture and understand what to focus on. All-in-One Product Management Made Effortless

title: Easy to Learn and Visually Appealing Platform
reviewText:
So far, it has been easy to learn how to use and aesthetically pleasing.
The feedback/research portal is not as fully developed as the rest of the platform; at times, it's a bit glitchy. I would also love to see the feedback portal have more ways to show related ideas for brainstorming.
I've used Craft for about a month, and the first problem that I wanted to solve was being able to easily create different versions of the roadmap for stakeholders, which Craft has allowed me to do with ease. I am now working to utilize the capacity feature to support overall planning, which is streamlining our processes and helping to facilitate conversations about priorities. Easy to Learn and Visually Appealing Platform

title: A clean and powerful Product Management tool that keeps teams aligned.
reviewText:
What I like most about Craft.io is how easy it is to work with right from day one. The interface feels clean and intuitive, so our team didn't need any heavy onboarding to get started. Even the implementation was smooth. Connecting it with jira and importing our existing backlog took only few minutes and didn't require any deep technical setup. The platform also comes with a solid set of features that actually support  real product workflows instead of adding clutter. Road mapping, prioritizing frameworks, feedback gathering, and documentation all live in one place, which keeps everything organized. I also really appreciate how well it integrates with tools we already use, like jira, slack and github. So, communication with the development team stays seamless. Their customers support has also been helpful and responsive whenever we has questions. We used Craft.io almost everyday... updating roadmaps, refining features and syncing with stakeholders and it has genuinely become a core part of how we operate.
If I had to pint out downsides, I'd say some of the more advanced features take time to fully understand, especially when customizing complex views of workflows. The implementation was overall easy but setting up deeper enterprise-level configurations like permissions and multi-team structures requires some trial and error. While customer support was very responsive but some technical questions sometimes take longer to resolve. Craft.io offers a lot of features, which is great but I do feel the analytics and reporting side could be more detailed compared to some completing tools. Since, this was a daily tool, there's a bit of a learning curve in making sure the whole team follows consistent processes otherwise it's easy for things to get messy.
We used Craft.io to manage our entire product lifecycle, from ideation and prioritizing to release tracking. It's our single source of truth for product roadmaps, customer feedback, and feature documentation. It keeps our design, dev, and business teams aligned without constant meetings or confusion. A clean and powerful Product Management tool that keeps teams aligned.

title: A Smart tool for Planning
reviewText:
Craft.io makes it easy to align product strategy with execution. I especially like how intuitive the interface is—it’s clean, well-organized, and helps me stay focused on priorities
I’ve found that some advanced customization options—especially around workflow templates and reporting—can be a bit limited. It would be great to have more flexibility in tailoring views or exporting data for broader stakeholder reporting
Craft.io helps solve the challenge of aligning product decisions with strategic goals. It centralizes product planning, making it easier to prioritize features, track progress, and communicate decisions clearly across teams. This has improved our decision-making process and reduced the time spent switching between tools or chasing updates. A Smart tool for Planning

title: Good Product Management tool
reviewText:
Craft.io is a great help for managing our product work. We use the tool with daily frequency to keep track of developments, and it has enough features to cover pretty much everything we need, it is in the sweet spot between simple and powerful. It is very easy to use, with a clean and straightforward UI, so getting started did not take long. Integrating it with our company process was smooth too, which is a big plus to onboard when you start to use it. In this phase, we particularly appreciated the role of customer support too, quick and effective.
Sometimes the app is a bit slow, especially when handling lots of data. It is not a dealbreaker, but it can be frustrating sometimes.
Dealing with the complexity of creating (and evolving) a new product. Keeping track of the product evolution and create a knowledge management repository. Spread product requirements across the product and development teams. Good Product Management tool
switchedReason: Asana was really stiff, Craft helped us to achieve the flexibility we were searching for. Asana’s structure sometimes made it hard to customize workflows or adapt quickly when priorities shifted. Craft help us to set workflows, customize views, and organize our features in a way that made sense for our team.

title: Roadmapping Made Easy
reviewText:
Craft.io stands out for its ability to make roadmapping functionality easy. The platform makes it incredibly easy to visualize, structure, and communicate product strategies across multiple levels — from high-level initiatives down to granular tasks. The ability to customize views and align roadmaps with objectives is particularly helpful for keeping stakeholders informed and engaged.

Additionally, the user interface is intuitive and easy to navigate. New users can get up and running quickly, and experienced product managers will appreciate how fluidly the UI supports complex workflows. The clean design and logical structure make for a pleasant and efficient user experience.

Another highlight is the excellent customer support, particularly during onboarding. Our representative, Amos, was responsive, knowledgeable, and genuinely invested in helping us succeed with the platform. Their guidance made the initial setup smooth and set us up for long-term success.
One notable gap is the lack of direct integration with Confluence. For teams that rely heavily on Confluence for documentation and knowledge sharing, this missing link creates friction in maintaining a seamless workflow across tools and causes less than ideal workarounds to be created.

Another shortcoming is how dates from workspace-level initiatives don’t sync cleanly with the portfolio-level view. This misalignment can lead to confusion or manual overhead when trying to ensure consistency across strategic layers. It would be helpful if date synchronization was more automatic and transparent across hierarchies.
Craft.io helps solve a core challenge many product teams face: connecting strategy to execution in a way that’s clear, visible, and scalable. Before using Craft, aligning roadmap items with business objectives and keeping cross-functional teams on the same page was a manual and disjointed process. Craft has streamlined that by centralizing our product planning, prioritization, and communication into one coherent system.

As a result, I’m seeing real benefits in terms of greater team alignment, and clearer visibility for stakeholders. It’s significantly reduced the time spent updating spreadsheets or presentation decks, and allowed me to focus more on strategic planning and product delivery. Roadmapping Made Easy
switchedReason:
We previously used Jira Product Discovery, but found that it lacked the depth and flexibility we needed for comprehensive product planning. While it served as a lightweight tool for idea capture and prioritization, it didn’t scale well for cross-team roadmapping or strategic alignment across initiatives. The integration with core Jira workflows was helpful, but ultimately it felt limiting when trying to manage a broader product portfolio.

Craft.io offered a more purpose-built experience for end-to-end product management, with much stronger support for roadmaps, goals, dependencies, and portfolio-level visibility. The structure of Craft better mirrors how we think about product work — from initiatives to features to tasks — and gives us the flexibility to work in a way that suits our team while still keeping leadership informed at the strategic level.

Making the switch has improved our planning accuracy, increased transparency across teams, and given us better tooling to align day-to-day work with long-term product strategy.

title: Amazing onboarding experience - Thank you!
reviewText:
The onboarding experience was awesome and a new standard.  One of the reasons we have struggled in the past with product management tools are getting out of the tool what we need with little or no guidance and working with Craft has been the complete opposite experience.
Nothing so far, the one piece of feedback I provided already had a solution!
Allowing us to manage and provide views at different altitudes for different audiences Amazing onboarding experience - Thank you!
switchedReason: There was no customization for prioiritization

title: When you’re looking for tools that help improve team processes and make things easier it is solution
reviewText:
Ease of Use

new frequent features

don' need to to have 5+ tools for product's and teams' processes When you’re looking for tools that help improve team processes and make things easier it is solution

title: Ferramenta simples, intuitiva e de fácil manutenção.
reviewText:
I managed to give a lot of visibility to my direction on our product's roadmap, the tool is simple, integrates with our development system, avoiding rework, I am loving using it.
The only point that could be improved is the language, both the tool and the videos and documentation are in English, it would be interesting if they were in Portuguese.
Roadmap management and visibility for the direction. Previously, I managed and reported everything in spreadsheets, now everything is located in the tool, I just send a link to everyone and they can get a simple and quick view of everything we have planned, what we are executing, etc. Ferramenta simples, intuitiva e de fácil manutenção.

title: A must-have tool for Product Managers
reviewText:
I've been using Craft on a daily basis for the past few months.
Supports simple to quite complex projects, that span across business segments.
Very easy to add new fields and shape the data the way you want them
Very easy to create new views and slice & dice the data in multiple ways
UI is easy to understand and navigate

Has improved the way we manage the backlog, prioritize items, prepare the committed and uncommited roadmap and have a consolidated view across all our business segments.
Bidirectional Jira integration, that can work across different Jira Projects.

Implementation was been straight forward from Craft's side. Delays were due to us having to re-think our processes and align with best practices
The customer success team have been absolutely fabulous so far, going the extra mile on many occasions.
Also Craft have been very responsive in our requests for new features / enhancements.
I would not say I dislike the following items, but mostly that they could be improved in future versions:

- Sorting on multiple fields in a table view
- Making custom-fields mandatory
- Searching data across custom fields in a table view
- Prohibiting the deletion of items, once they have been synced to Jira
- Alerting Product Managers on specific events (for example when a date slips)
- Workflows for effort approval
Craft is here to 
- act as the single source of truth for all Product Management tasks (backlog, prioritization, roadmaps, specifications, aligning work with the company strategy) 
- make it easy to create and share a common view of the roadmap, targeted at different audiences (internal / external) and presented at different levels of detail (high-level Initiatives, Solutions, Features)
- collect Feedback from various teams and tie it to new Product features

Craft is helping the entiere organization, to instantly have a clear view on what we are building and why. A must-have tool for Product Managers
